name: Aho-Corasick
def: Matches many patterns at once in linear time using a trie augmented with failure links.
problem: Scan text once for thousands of keywords simultaneously (filters, IDS, dictionaries).
mechanism: Build a trie of all patterns, add failure links (like KMP across the trie), then run the text through the automaton emitting all matches.
benefits: O(n + matches) for any number of patterns
limits: Automaton memory grows with pattern set
failures: Memory blow-up with huge pattern sets
myths: "Just run KMP per pattern" — that is O(k·n); Aho-Corasick is one pass
